title: Cancel a paid order before shipment
summary: Allow an authenticated customer to cancel an owned paid order before shipment and request one refund.

stakeholder_intent.actor: Authenticated customer who owns the order
stakeholder_intent.goal: Cancel a paid order before it is shipped
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Avoid unwanted fulfillment while preserving a deterministic refund process
stakeholder_intent.trigger: The customer selects Cancel order on an eligible order
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: The order is cancelled and one refund request is recorded

in_scope: Cancel an owned order in PAID state

preconditions: The order exists and belongs to the requester
The order is in PAID state

business_rules.rule: Only the owning customer may cancel the order
A paid order may be cancelled only before shipment
business_rules.outcome: Cancel PAID orders and reject SHIPPED orders

business_rules.rule: At most one refund request may exist for the payment
business_rules.outcome: Reuse the cancellation id as the refund idempotency key

outputs.body: Order representation with status CANCELLED and refundStatus REQUESTED

errors.condition: Requester does not own the order
errors.message: Order is not owned by the requester

side_effects.description: Publish RefundRequested after the order cancellation commits
side_effects.idempotency: Use the cancellation id as the event and consumer idempotency key
side_effects.failure_policy: Persist through the existing transactional outbox and retry with the standard policy

security.authentication: Use the existing customer session middleware
authorization_rules: The authenticated customer id must equal order.customerId
audit_requirements: Record actor id, order id, timestamp, and cancellation outcome

architecture_constraints: HTTP handlers call application use cases and never repositories directly
allowed_changes: Add the cancellation use case, endpoint, tests, and refund event contract
forbidden_changes: Do not change payment settlement behavior
dependencies: Reuse the existing application clock and transaction manager
implementation_context.migration_strategy: No schema migration is required because cancellation fields already exist
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Lock or version-check the order inside the existing transaction and reject a concurrent shipment transition

acceptance_scenarios.title: Owner cancels a paid order
given: An authenticated customer owns an order in PAID state
when: The customer calls the cancellation endpoint

acceptance_scenarios.title: Non-owner cannot cancel the order
given: An authenticated customer does not own the order
when: The customer calls the cancellation endpoint
then: No state or side effect is produced

test_cases: Integration-test atomic order cancellation and outbox insertion
non_functional_requirements: A repeated cancellation request must not create another refund request
definition_of_done: No forbidden module dependency is introduced

decision_log.decision: Implement cancellation as an application use case called by the HTTP handler
decision_log.rationale: This follows the existing order-module dependency direction

decision_log.decision: Publish RefundRequested through the transactional outbox
decision_log.rationale: This preserves the existing transaction and retry model

decision_log.decision: Only paid, unshipped orders can be cancelled by their owner
decision_log.rationale: This is the confirmed product rule for the slice

codebase_evidence.finding: Existing handlers delegate to application use cases and do not access repositories directly
The outbox is written inside the application transaction and retried asynchronously
